findings.title: Service/admin key shipped to the client (RLS bypass)
findings.snippet: README claim matches red flag: A fun little project. Fully serverless, no backend needed — it talks to Supabase

findings.title: Service/admin key shipped to the client (RLS bypass)
findings.snippet: README claim matches red flag: Just add your service role key to .env and you're done. No login required, the API

findings.title: API endpoint / route handler with no authentication check
findings.snippet: README claim matches red flag: Just add your service role key to .env and you're done. No login required, the API

findings.title: Secret inlined into the built JS bundle or leaked via source maps
findings.snippet: README claim matches red flag: Deploy anywhere: 100% client-side, host on any static CDN.

findings.title: RLS disabled on a table (public API fully open)
findings.snippet: ALTER TABLE public.users DISABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
findings.fix: Enable RLS on every table exposed via the anon key and add an explicit owner-scoped policy.

findings.title: pull_request_target builds untrusted PR code
findings.fix: pull_request_target runs with secrets. Don't checkout/build/run the PR head under it; split trusted/untrusted jobs.

findings.title: Untrusted GitHub event field interpolated into run:
findings.fix: Pass the field through an env: var and reference $VAR in run:, never inline ${{ github.event... }}.

findings.title: Untrusted GitHub event field interpolated into run:
findings.fix: Pass the field through an env: var and reference $VAR in run:, never inline ${{ github.event... }}.

findings.title: Firebase security rule allows anyone to read/write
findings.fix: Replace `if true` with `if request.auth != null && request.auth.uid == resource.data.ownerId` (scope to the owner).

findings.title: Firebase security rule allows anyone to read/write
findings.fix: Replace `if true` with `if request.auth != null && request.auth.uid == resource.data.ownerId` (scope to the owner).

findings.title: Key / credential file committed to the repo
findings.fix: Remove from the tree, add to .gitignore, rotate the credential, and purge from git history.

findings.title: Key / credential file committed to the repo
findings.fix: Remove from the tree, add to .gitignore, rotate the credential, and purge from git history.

findings.title: Cloudflare .dev.vars (local secrets) committed
findings.fix: Gitignore .dev.vars and *.local; put Workers secrets via `wrangler secret put` / Secret bindings, not committed files or wrangler [vars].

findings.title: Slash command / agent grants unrestricted tools
findings.fix: Scope allowed-tools to the exact commands the command needs (e.g. Bash(git status:*)); flag only Bash(*), a bare unscoped Bash, or a literal * (all tools). Scoped globs like Bash(node:*) are correct and not flagged.

findings.title: RLS policy with a permanently-true condition
findings.fix: A policy of using(true) is the same as no policy. Scope to auth.uid() = owner_id (or your tenant column).

findings.title: RLS policy with a permanently-true condition
findings.snippet: CREATE POLICY "open_write" ON public.posts WITH CHECK (true);
findings.fix: A policy of using(true) is the same as no policy. Scope to auth.uid() = owner_id (or your tenant column).

findings.title: Workflow grants write-all / broad token permissions
findings.fix: Set least-privilege `permissions:` per job; default to contents: read.

findings.title: Third-party GitHub Action pinned to a mutable ref
findings.fix: Pin third-party actions to a full commit SHA; a mutable tag/branch can be moved to malicious code.

findings.title: MCP config / plugin points at an unpinned remote server
findings.fix: Pin MCP server packages to an exact version and trust the source; an unpinned/remote server can change under you.

findings.title: Secret hardcoded in MCP / plugin config
findings.fix: Reference secrets via ${ENV_VAR}; don't paste live keys into committed MCP config.

findings.title: Committed mcp.json forwards host secrets / uses editor var substitution
findings.fix: Don't forward host secrets into a committed mcp-config env; pin server packages; gate auto-launch behind explicit trust.

findings.title: settings.json widens permissions / auto-approves dangerous tools
findings.fix: Don't ship bypassPermissions or Bash(*) allows; grant the narrowest permission set the project needs.

findings.title: Committed agent config runs code / redirects API on open
findings.fix: Don't ship repo-local SessionStart hooks, base-URL overrides, or auto-enabled MCP servers; require explicit per-repo trust.

findings.title: Committed agent config runs code / redirects API on open
findings.fix: Don't ship repo-local SessionStart hooks, base-URL overrides, or auto-enabled MCP servers; require explicit per-repo trust.

findings.title: Gemini CLI config runs code on session start
findings.fix: Remove SessionStart hooks from committed .gemini/settings.json; require explicit per-repo trust.

findings.title: Committed agent config runs code / redirects API on open
findings.fix: Don't ship repo-local SessionStart hooks, base-URL overrides, or auto-enabled MCP servers; require explicit per-repo trust.

findings.title: Cursor rule file forces injection on every prompt
findings.fix: Review and remove alwaysApply:true from committed .cursor/rules/*.mdc files; rules should require explicit opt-in.

findings.title: VS Code tasks.json runs code on folder open
findings.fix: Remove runOn:folderOpen from committed tasks.json; never auto-execute commands on project open in a shared repo.

findings.title: Firebase test-mode rules shipped (time-bomb open access)
findings.snippet: allow read, write: if request.time < timestamp.date(2030, 12, 31);
findings.fix: Test-mode rules open the DB until a date then lock it. Replace with real auth/ownership rules before launch.

findings.title: Browser extension requests all-URLs host permissions
findings.fix: Scope host_permissions and content-script matches to the exact origins needed; <all_urls> lets one compromise touch every site.

findings.title: Browser extension requests all-URLs host permissions
findings.fix: Scope host_permissions and content-script matches to the exact origins needed; <all_urls> lets one compromise touch every site.

findings.title: Android app shipped debuggable
findings.fix: Never ship android:debuggable=true; it lets anyone attach a debugger and read app memory and data on a real device.

findings.title: Mobile app allows cleartext (HTTP) traffic
findings.fix: Disable cleartext: usesCleartextTraffic=false with a locked network-security-config; NSAllowsArbitraryLoads=false. Cleartext enables MITM.

findings.title: Dotenv package bundles .env file into the app binary
findings.fix: Never bundle real secrets in a mobile app via dotenv. Use a backend proxy for sensitive API calls; only include publishable/anon keys in the client.

findings.title: package.json postinstall runs remote / opaque code
findings.fix: Remove network/remote-exec from install scripts; review any dependency that needs them.

findings.title: Tauri allowlist grants broad fs/shell access
findings.fix: Enable only the Tauri APIs actually used and scope fs/shell to required paths; 'all: true' hands the webview full host power.

findings.title: Terraform state committed (often holds plaintext secrets)
findings.fix: Never commit tfstate. Use a remote backend (S3+DynamoDB, TF Cloud), gitignore it, and rotate any secrets it exposed.

findings.title: Extension exposes web_accessible_resources to all sites
findings.fix: List specific resources and restrict matches to your own origins; a broad web_accessible_resources enables fingerprinting and abuse.

findings.title: Custom URL scheme deep link hijackable by any installed app
findings.fix: Use App Links (https:// with autoVerify + assetlinks.json) or Universal Links instead of custom schemes for auth callbacks.

findings.title: Next.js image remotePatterns wildcard host
findings.fix: Restrict images.remotePatterns to the exact hostnames you serve; a ** hostname lets the optimizer fetch arbitrary hosts.

findings.title: Source maps present in build output
findings.fix: Don't ship .map files to production; they reverse your minified bundle (and sometimes leak comments/paths).

findings.title: Android allowBackup enabled (data extractable via adb)
findings.fix: Set android:allowBackup=false (plus backup rules) for apps holding tokens/PII; allowBackup lets `adb backup` pull app data.

note: Scanner output is candidate findings. Confirm each at file:line before reporting; drop false positives (comments, tests, docs, safe public keys).
